metadata.draft_title: USB4 v2 vs Thunderbolt 5: A Technical Analysis

ambiguous_claims.text: Making it suitable for charging more demanding devices and future-proofing setups with high-power standards. [2] In contrast, USB4 V2. 0 supports a maximum of 25 watts natively, but can reach up to 240 watts when using USB Power Delivery.

This ensures continuity for devices outside of the high-end market segment. [1] Thunderbolt 5's mandatory minimum speed is higher, And its power delivery capabilities are significantly more robust due to its adoption of advanced USB Power Delivery protocols via EPR.

verified_claims.text: USB4 requires a minimum of 7. 5W power output, while Thunderbolt 5 requires a minimum of 140W.
In practical terms, Thunderbolt 5 has a minimum mandatory speed of 80 Gbit/s, whereas USB4 V2. 0 only mandates 20 Gbit/s.

In practical terms, Although Thunderbolt 5 also supports asymmetrical speeds up to 120 Gbit/s for display transmissions. [2] [1] In practical terms, In practical terms, In terms of power delivery, Thunderbolt 5 is designed to support significantly higher wattage than USB4 V2. 0, While USB4 V2. 0 relies on the USB Type-C specification and requires a Downstream Facing Port (DFP) to supply at least 7. 5 W Type-C current.
Thunderbolt 5 demands a minimum charging power of 140W, a substantial increase from Thunderbolt 4's 100W, In practical terms. [1] [2], In practical terms.
This enhanced power delivery capability in Thunderbolt 5 is achieved through its adoption of the latest USB Power Delivery protocol via USB Extended Power Range (EPR).

The adoption of EPR in Thunderbolt 5 enables it to support higher power output capabilities compared to USB4 V2. 0. [1] [2] In practical terms, Both technologies leverage the USB-C connector and are designed for efficient charging solutions, catering to users with power-hungry gadgets, 0 in 2024.
With Intel not charging license fees for the use of Thunderbolt technology, However. [2] Manufacturers must still have their devices certified in a laboratory to use the Thunderbolt logo.
May be able to cope with the data rate of Thunderbolt 5, And that Thunderbolt 4 will not be rendered obsolete by the new version.

This makes Thunderbolt 5 a more powerful option for users requiring high-bandwidth data transfers and substantial power delivery for their devices. [2] In practical terms, For users with power-hungry gadgets, , In contrast, USB4 V2.

Although it can reach higher powers with USB Power Delivery. [2] This makes USB4 V2. 0 a more accessible option for general users who do not require the extreme power delivery or high-speed data transfer capabilities of Thunderbolt 5.
